global_cues.us_markets: Friday Apr 24 close: S&P 500 +0.80% to 7,165.08 (record high), Nasdaq +1.63% to 24,836.60 (record high), Dow -0.2% to 49,230.71. Strong tech/semiconductor rally led by Intel (+23%, best day since dot-com era), AMD (+14%), and Nvidia retaking $5T market cap. Semis up 18 straight sessions.
global_cues.european_markets: European indices firm into the weekend on extended US-Iran ceasefire and easing oil; risk-on tone consistent with Wall Street records.
global_cues.asian_markets: GIFT Nifty trading ~24,134, up ~0.75% / +180 pts vs prior close, signaling a gap-up open for Indian equities. Nikkei 225 and Hang Seng reflecting positive bias following US tech surge.

fii_dii_summary: Apr 24, 2026 cash segment: FIIs net SELLERS at -₹8,827.87 cr (heavy outflow), DIIs net BUYERS at +₹4,700.71 cr. DII absorption only partially offsets FII selling. Trend remains FII-cautious on rupee weakness and crude pressure; DIIs continue to provide cushion.
earnings_calendar: SBI Cards and Payment Services - Apr 27
Kotak Mahindra Bank - week of Apr 28
macro_events: RBI MPC (Apr 8) held repo rate unchanged at 5.25%, neutral stance; SDF 5.00%, MSF 5.50% — 2nd consecutive pause
March CPI inflation at 3.4% YoY, well below RBI tolerance band; FY27 CPI projected at 4.6%, GDP at 6.9%
RBI reaffirmed 4% inflation target with 2-6% band for FY26-FY31
208 companies reporting Q4 FY26 earnings between Apr 27 - May 2; peak earnings week
RBI April 2026 monthly bulletin published Apr 24 with policy updates
sector_themes: Pharma: rebounding as US generics tariff fears cool and Budget 2026 ₹10,000 cr biopharma plan supports sentiment — selectively positive
IT: avoid fresh short-term buying; sector under pressure
Banking/Financials: in focus with SBI Cards, AU SFB, Kotak, Bajaj Finance results this week — Q4 earnings will set tone
Cement: UltraTech, ACC results key bellwethers for capex/demand cycle
Energy & Metals: preferred space given crude strength and inflation hedge
Auto: Maruti Suzuki Q4 ahead; Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles newly admitted to NSE Capital Market segment Apr 23
FMCG: HUL, DMart, Varun Beverages results to indicate consumption trends
Adani group: Total Gas, Power, Ports, ACC all reporting this week — group-level focus
risk_flags: USD/INR at ~94.2, four-week low, rupee down ~1.3% this week (5th straight session of weakness) — FII outflow risk
Brent crude $104-108, holding above $105; India imports ~85% of crude — direct CAD/inflation pressure
Strait of Hormuz remains closed despite US-Iran ceasefire extension (Apr 21) — supply risk premium intact, ceasefire fragile
Heavy FII selling (-₹8,828 cr Friday) signals foreign caution amid currency/crude headwinds
Domestic indices fell ~2% last week despite global record highs — divergence risk if FII selling persists
Earnings concentration risk: 48 companies report Apr 27 alone, stock-specific volatility likely
Geopolitical: West Asia conflict flagged by RBI as 'unprecedented challenge'; Iran retains drones, sovereign transit fee dispute unresolved
brief_summary: Setup is constructive but mixed: GIFT Nifty signals a gap-up open on Wall Street's record-high tech rally, yet last week's 2% drop in Sensex/Nifty, heavy FII selling (-₹8,828 cr Friday), rupee at 94.2 (4-week low) and Brent above $105 cap upside. Expect a positive open faded into selling unless FII flows turn; stay selective into a heavy earnings week (Coal India, UltraTech, SBI Cards, Adani Total Gas Apr 27; Maruti, HUL, Kotak, Bajaj Finance later in week). Favor pharma/energy/metals, avoid fresh IT longs, and watch USD/INR and crude as the dominant macro drivers.
